message:
So I'm standing in my kitchen at 11 PM last week, reheating leftovers, when I realize I haven't actually *looked* at my smoke alarm in... honestly, I don't know how long. I grab a chair, climb up there, and it's basically a dust colony. I start laughing because here I am, supposedly an adult who pays taxes, and I've been living under what's essentially a decorative plastic disc.

Five minutes and one gentle cleaning later, I tested it. Worked perfectly. (Obviously it did—it's not broken, I'm just lazy.)

The weird thing? That tiny moment of "oh, I should probably know where everyone sleeps" or "wait, do we have a plan if we can't use the front door?" suddenly felt way less boring than it used to. Turns out you don't need a disaster to motivate you—sometimes just catching yourself *not* being ready is enough.

Does anyone else have that one thing you keep meaning to handle but somehow never do? What finally gets you to actually do it? 😄
